summary: Matched a large batch of video intro/cutscene functions across the vi module, especially vi1201v1.c, vi1202.c, vi0801.c, vi0601.c, vi1201v2.c, vi0501.c, vi0502.c, and vi0102.c. The PR also tightened cross-module prototypes and extern declarations in gr, pl, mn, it, and ft headers needed by the newly matched vi code. Review feedback specifically called out raw byte/offset access patterns and recommended pulling them into structs, preferably, or using M2C_FIELD.
what_changed: Replaced multiple vi module placeholder functions with C implementations for video intro/cutscene setup, camera callbacks, per-frame callbacks, archive loads, fog/light/camera/model GObj creation, and animation sequencing.
Implemented or expanded OnEnter-style cutscene entry points such as un_8031DE58_OnEnter in vi0501.c and un_8031FD18_OnEnter in vi1201v1.c.
Expanded vi1202.c with gameplay/crowd-related sequence logic including un_8032233C and refactoring around knockback category thresholds from gCrowdConfig.

Changed vi0102 camera rendering from a vi_RunCamera helper call to explicit HSD_CObjSetCurrent, HSD_SetEraseColor, HSD_CObjEraseScreen, vi_8031CA04, gxlink priority update, HSD_GObj_80390ED0, and HSD_CObjEndCurrent calls.
Moved some symbols to more appropriate storage and linkage, for example making vi0102_RunFrame static and making initial_pos static in vi0102.c.
Added or corrected static/header externs needed by vi functions, including scene/archive/static frame symbols in vi0501.static.h.

Corrected cross-module function prototypes used by vi code, including grBigBlueRoute_8020DAB4(char*, float, int), grCorneria_801DDD4C(Vec3*), grCorneria_801E1BF0(void), grCorneria_801E2E50(enum_t), grCorneria_801E2EA0(void), mnCharSel_8025D1C4(int, int), mnCharSel_80264070(void), and pl_8003FDA0(int, int).

Changed it_804D6D40 from an uncertain f32** declaration with comments to f32*, and moved extern declarations for it_804D6D40 into item headers for Leadead and Octarock rather than local C-file externs.
Included formatting and include cleanup in item headers and item source files such as itdosei.c, itlucky.h, and itwhispyapple.h.
The author noted in discussion that broken or regressed matches would be fixed, indicating some intermediate matching regressions were found during review or CI.
smart_moves: Used cross-module prototype refinement to make vi matches possible instead of preserving broad UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS declarations where the call sites revealed argument and return types.

Converted repeated raw scene setup into concrete HSD object creation flows: archive symbol loading, fog/light/camera/model GObj creation, HSD object attachment, GX links, animation requests, and GObj procs.
Matched camera callback behavior by expanding helper-like logic into the exact low-level HSD_CObj sequence when that better reflected the target code.

Introduced or reused specific domain types such as Vec3, HSD_GObj*, DynamicsDesc*, enum_t, SceneDesc*, HSD_Archive*, and CrowdConfig rather than leaving all cross-module interfaces as opaque placeholders.
Centralized global extern declarations in headers for shared symbols like it_804D6D40, reducing repeated local externs in item source files.
Preserved decomp project style with address comments on declarations while refining types.
decomp_lessons: When vi/cutscene code calls into stage, player, menu, item, or fighter systems, matching often requires correcting those systems' headers first; broad UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS prototypes can block or distort matches.

OnEnter cutscene functions commonly combine audio reset/setup, effect library initialization, archive symbol loading, HSD fog/light/camera/model object creation, animation request/advance, and proc installation; future vi work should compare against this pattern.

If a function receives an opaque void* or u8* input blob, identify whether it is really a structured argument object. Review explicitly preferred pulling fields into a struct over continuing raw byte access.
Raw byte writes like un_804D6F84[0], [1], and [3] can be an improvement over M2C_FIELD for simple arrays, but larger or semantically meaningful blobs should become named structs.
Avoid speculative pointer-depth declarations for globals when the matching evidence supports a simpler type; this PR changed it_804D6D40 from uncertain f32** to f32*.

Small signature changes can propagate broadly: adding an unused argument to pl_8003FDA0 or changing grCorneria returns from UNK_RET to bool/float/HSD_GObj* may be necessary for vi call-site correctness.
Local helpers that are not exported should be made static after matching, as shown by vi0102_RunFrame.
naming_conventions: Video module files use viXXXX.c naming for sequence IDs, for example vi0102, vi0401, vi0402, vi0501, vi0502, vi0601, vi0801, vi1101, vi1201v1, vi1201v2, and vi1202.
Many unmatched or partially named functions retain address-based names such as un_8031DE58_OnEnter, fn_8031DD14, vi_8031DC80, and un_8032233C.
Scene entry functions use an OnEnter suffix when they are scene/state entry points, for example un_8031DE58_OnEnter and un_8031FD18_OnEnter.
Frame callbacks use OnFrame suffixes where known, for example vi_8031E0F0_OnFrame.
Static globals retain address-based names such as un_804D6F70, un_804D6F74, un_804D6F78, un_804DE070, un_804D7050, and un_804D6F84.
Header declarations preserve address comments in the form /* 31DE58 */ before symbols.
Function names in stage/item/player/menu modules retained subsystem prefixes such as grCorneria_, grBigBlueRoute_, it_, mnCharSel_, and pl_.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use explicit HSD_CObj current/erase/end-current calls when helper abstraction changes codegen or ordering, as in vi0102_CameraCallback.
For matching float constants and frame triggers, introduce static f32 symbols when the binary appears to load from data instead of using literals directly, as seen with un_804DE048 and un_804DE070.
Use temporary locals to influence register allocation and ordering, such as assigning un_804D6F58 to a local ptr and asserting before copying Vec3 data in vi0401.c.
Represent 64-bit audio masks carefully; the diff changed a literal to lbAudioAx_8002702C(8, (u64) 0x100 << 32), likely to match expression/codegen expectations.
When M2C_FIELD is present around known simple storage, replace it with typed access if evidence supports it, such as un_804D6F84 byte array indexing.
For unknown data blobs with repeated offset access, review guidance says to define a struct where possible, or use M2C_FIELD if a struct cannot yet be justified.
Introduce real prototypes for callees so argument passing and return-value use match the target, for example grCorneria_801E2EA0 returning float and grCorneria_801E1BF0 returning HSD_GObj*.

review_feedback.comment: pull out into a struct (preferred) or use M2C_FIELD
review_feedback.lesson: The implemented un_8031FD18_OnEnter treated the void* argument as a u8* input and accessed byte offsets directly; reviewer guidance preferred a typed struct for the input data or M2C_FIELD as a fallback.

review_feedback.comment: pull out into struct (preferred) or use M2C_FIELD
review_feedback.lesson: The un_8032233C/un_803224DC area used raw offset access such as casting mpLib_80458868 to char* and reading *(f32*) (mp + 0x18); reviewer guidance preferred a struct for that data layout.

review_feedback.comment: oops, will fix the broken/regressed matches
review_feedback.lesson: The PR had at least some broken or regressed matches during iteration; exact functions are not identified in the provided slice.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that define structs for the vi1201v1 OnEnter input blob or vi1202/mpLib_80458868 data layout.
Search for subsequent changes to un_8031FD18_OnEnter and un_8032233C to see whether review feedback about structs or M2C_FIELD was applied.
Audit vi module functions that still use raw u8* input argument access and compare with the struct guidance from PR #2073.
Check whether it_804D6D40 remained f32* in later item decomp work or was refined to a named data structure.
Search for vi camera callbacks that can reuse the explicit HSD_CObjSetCurrent/HSD_CObjEraseScreen/HSD_CObjEndCurrent matching pattern from vi0102_CameraCallback.
